title: Funastrum rotundifolium
intro: Funastrum rotundifolium, also known as Round-leaved Brugmansia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Funastrum rotundifolium include alkaloids, such as scaponin and funasterone, which have been found to possess immunomodulatory and antioxidant activities. In herbal medicine, Funastrum rotundifolium is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional remedies, such as infusions and decoctions, to promote relaxation and relieve pain.
taxonomy: Funastrum rotundifolium is classified within the family Apocynaceae and is a species of flowering plant in the genus Funastrum. This plant is native to the Americas, specifically in regions from Mexico to South America. Funastrum rotundifolium is often associated with the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which is a group of plants characterized by their milky sap. The plant's classification also places it in the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Overall, its classification within these categories provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
